evidence: The presence of a white vehicle roof with a yellow strap is a well-known Google Street View car meta-clue unique to certain regions of Brazil.
Double yellow center lines are the standard road marking for two-way traffic separation in Brazil.
Red/orange soil (Terra Roxa) combined with flat, vast agricultural fields is highly characteristic of the Cerrado biome in the Brazilian states of Mato Grosso, Mato Grosso do Sul, and Goiás.
The hazy or smoky sky and dry vegetation suggest the 'queimadas' (burning) season typical of the Brazilian interior during the dry months.
The lattice electric transmission tower and large deciduous trees fit the infrastructure and flora of the Brazilian central-west.
